title: Formatting SQL Queries - Security Sensitive
description: The way SQL queries are formatted can have significant security implications. Improper neutralization of special elements used in an SQL command can lead to SQL injection, improper input validation, and other critical vulnerabilities. This rule is designed to identify potential issues related to SQL command formatting.
recommendation: Use PreparedStatement or Parameter Binding to escape inputs for SQL queries and prevent potential SQL injection vulnerabilities.
impact: Improper handling of this rule can lead to SQL Injection, which is a critical security vulnerability that can lead to unauthorized access to data and potential data loss.

noncompliant: org.hibernate.Query query2 =  session.createQuery("FROM students where fname = " + data);

references.title: CWE-89 - Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command

checks.message: Use PreparedStatement or Parameter Binding to escape inputs for SQL queries and prevent potential SQL injection vulnerabilities.
